Post-dominant code review for bundle-436-438 (#436 D-419 P1 + #438 D-419 P3).
Reviewed all n1-n4 implement-node changes against the seven critical criteria.

== Critical criteria results ==

1. [INV-2] byte-identity (open-next MUST NOT write running-set.json): PASS.
   Verified directly in runOpenNext (scripts/kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js:1143-1260):
   the only writeFile target is planPath (line 1201). No runningSetPath / running-set
   reference in the function body. Test D419-INV2 confirms (asserts no file ending in
   RUNNING_SET_NAME is written; passes).

2. Close-node field-survival: PASS. All THREE empty-set fallbacks now spread the
   existing object instead of a bare literal:
     - runCloseAndOpenNext (line ~1473): { ...running, state: 'open', nodes: [] }
     - runCloseNode (line ~2585): { ...running, state: 'open', nodes: [] }
     - runReconcileRunningSet (line ~2705): { ...running, state: 'open', nodes: [] }
   grep confirms zero remaining bare { state: 'open', nodes: [] } without ...running.
   Test D419-CLOSE-FIELDSURVIVAL asserts max_concurrent=3 + extra_field survive; passes.

3. max_concurrent correctness: PASS.
   - runOpenReady: maxConcurrent = Number.isInteger(max) && max>=1 ? Math.min(cap,max) : cap
     (set at OPEN time; cap = fanoutCapReadonly||8). Recorded in openingSet (state:'opening')
     and survives Phase-3 promotion via ...openingSet spread.
   - NOT in plan_hash: max_concurrent appears only in running-set.json, never in planContent;
     absent from plan-validator.js and adaptive-schema.js entirely (plan_hash covers Meta+Nodes).
   - runReconcileRunningSet caps roll-forward at budget = max(0, ceiling - liveStable.length);
     ceiling falls back to 1 (fail-closed) when max_concurrent absent/invalid. cappedOut nodes
     get baselines dropped (--drop-base) and are excluded from survivors. Test D419-INV7 confirms
     (max_concurrent=2, 1 stable live, 2 opening -> only 1 rolls forward; final nodes <= 2).

4. 'frontier unit' pin preservation: PASS. All six plan-run surfaces retain the literal
   string (grep -c == 4 on each of the 6 surfaces).

5. parallel_safe prohibition: PASS. All four planner surfaces (agents/workflow-planner.md +
   three workflow-planner.toml) state explicitly: "NEVER add parallel_safe to a node
   yourself — it is validator-derived ([INV-17]); adding it by hand produces an
   invalid_annotation refusal."

6. Four-chain green: PASS. Ran sequentially from the worktree:
     claude=0  codex=0  gitlab=0  gitea=0
   test-adaptive-node.js: 503 assertions passed (incl. D419-INV2 / D419-INV7 /
   D419-CLOSE-FIELDSURVIVAL).

7. Forge-neutral: PASS. The new D-419 prose on the six plan-run surfaces and four planner
   files carries no forge-specific behavioral tokens. The only gitlab/gitea/github matches
   are pre-existing cross-edition registration/port-path references (e.g.
   kaola-gitlab-workflow-<base>.js, generated_port_split, agent-registration surface) — none
   added by this diff (git diff of added lines for forge tokens is empty).

== Cross-edition parity ==
   - plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/...adaptive-node.js byte-identical to root.
   - gitlab/gitea edition ports carry the same D-419-01 changes (max_concurrent / cappedOut /
     liveStable occurrence counts match root: 10 / 4 / 2).
   - architecture.md (n2) documents INV-2 byte-identity, max_concurrent fail-closed-to-1
     semantics, and the reconcile budget formula (max_concurrent - already-live), consistent
     with the code.

== Consistency note (non-blocking) ==
   architecture.md describes the reconcile budget as "max_concurrent - count-of-already-live
   nodes"; the code uses liveStable (stable non-opening in_progress members), which is the
   correct already-live set. Doc and code agree.

Verdict: APPROVE. No CRITICAL or HIGH findings. All seven critical criteria pass; four chains green.
